what_it_does: A Claude Code SessionStart hook that checks whether the stored capability-registry version stamp matches the live binary version, warns if the stamp is stale or missing, and always exits 0. It first attempts a registry-driven Python freshness scan, then falls back to a claude-code-only bash check.
observations: Wired as a non-blocking SessionStart hook via .claude/settings.json; emits warnings to stdout only when something is stale or unreachable.
Captures hook stdin for a self-marker write via write_self_marker.py, wrapped in a failure-isolated block so marker failures cannot break session start.
Uses environment overrides COMPANY_PYTHON and COMPANY_CLAUDE_BIN with sensible defaults.
First attempts registry_freshness.py across platforms/<id>.py files that declare a version_source; falls back to the original claude-code-only bash logic if the helper fails.
Never writes the stamp itself; only proposes refresh via warning text.
Defines a single shell function warn that prefixes messages with 'REGISTRY FRESHNESS:'.
standouts: Dual-path freshness logic: registry-driven Python helper plus a defensive bash fallback.
Failure-isolated self-marker block (#69) that explicitly must not break session-start.
Explicit non-blocking posture: every branch exits 0.
conventions: Uses COMPANY_ROOT computed from the script location.
Comment-heavy, almost literate style with section dividers and pattern notes.
Bash function naming lowercase; stdout warnings are consumed as Claude Code additionalContext.
concerns: The self-marker block runs a Python script on every session start; any bug in write_self_marker.py or its invocation could still consume time or produce hidden failures, despite the swallowing wrapper.
Fallback claude-code-only check hardcodes $HOME/.local/bin/claude as a secondary path.
LIVE_VERSION parsing relies on sed/awk heuristics that could drift if 'claude --version' output format changes.
No explicit validation that STAMPED_VERSION is non-empty before comparison, although the comparison still behaves safely.
notes: A defensive, read-only session-start sentinel that prefers a registry-wide Python freshness scan and degrades gracefully to a single-binary check.
questions: What is the exact JSON schema of the SessionStart hook stdin being captured?
What does registry_freshness.py return or print on success versus failure, and does it emit its own warnings?
What is the #69 self-marker and where is its output consumed?
What platforms declare version_source and how is 'stale' defined for each?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it warns about stale or missing registry stamps without blocking sessions, exactly as the header states.
apparent_themes: Governance separation (read-only warning versus write-approved refresh)
intention_signals: The file is trying to make session-start aware of registry freshness across all known platforms while guaranteeing that freshness-checking can never prevent a session from starting, and to leave stamp mutation to an approved refresh flow.
novelty: Combines a registry-wide Python freshness scan with a failure-isolated self-marker write inside a single SessionStart hook, and explicitly documents the 'first file in ops/hooks/' new-pattern status.
contribution: Provides a read-only, non-blocking session-start warning path that can detect stale or missing registry stamps and guide users toward refresh, with a fallback so the check survives helper unavailability.
summary_for_embedding: A non-blocking Claude Code SessionStart hook that checks the capability registry version stamp against the live binary, optionally runs a registry-wide Python freshness scan, writes a failure-isolated self-marker, and warns on stale or missing state without ever writing the stamp.
intention_for_embedding: To surface registry freshness issues at the start of every Claude Code session while guaranteeing session start is never blocked by the check.

symbols.does: Prefixes and prints a freshness warning to stdout.
symbols.description: Single shell helper used throughout the script to emit 'REGISTRY FRESHNESS: <message>' lines; stdout is consumed by Claude Code as additionalContext, so it intentionally produces output only when there is something to report.
